challenges.title: Set Comprehension
challenges.description: Given `words = ["hello", "world", "hello", "python", "world"]`, create a set comprehension called `unique_lengths` that contains the unique lengths of the words in the list.
challenges.starter_code:
words = ["hello", "world", "hello", "python", "world"]

# Create a set comprehension of unique word lengths
unique_lengths = None  # Replace with your code

print(unique_lengths)

challenges.title: Fibonacci Generator
challenges.description: Write a generator function `fibonacci(n)` that yields the first `n` Fibonacci numbers. The sequence starts with 0, 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, ...
challenges.starter_code:
def fibonacci(n):
    # Your code here
    pass

# Test your generator
print(list(fibonacci(7)))
print(list(fibonacci(1)))

challenges.title: Matrix Transpose
challenges.description: Given `matrix = [[1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6], [7, 8, 9]]`, create a variable `transposed` that contains the transpose of the matrix using a nested list comprehension. The transpose swaps rows and columns.
challenges.starter_code:
matrix = [[1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6], [7, 8, 9]]
